title: Nonea rosea
intro: Nonea rosea, also known as nonea or rose nonea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the Balkans and Mediterranean regions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for relieving digestive issues, reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Nonea rosea include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Nonea rosea is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Nonea rosea belongs to the family Boraginaceae. This plant is a part of the genus Nonea. The species is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Nonea rosea is a type of flowering plant, characterized by its vibrant blooms. It is typically found in well-drained, rocky areas at high elevations.
